instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God,
output: Principio del evangelio de Jesucristo, Hijo de Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Even as it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, who will prepare Your way,
output: Como está escrito en Isaías el profeta: “He aquí Yo envío Mi mensajero delante de Tu faz, el cual preparará Tu camino.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: A voice of one crying in the wilderness: Prepare the way of the Lord; make straight His paths.”
output: Voz de uno que clama en el desierto: Preparad el camino del Señor; enderezad Sus sendas”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.
output: Apareció Juan en el desierto bautizando y predicando el bautismo de arrepentimiento para perdón de pecados.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And all the region of Judea went out to him, and all the people of Jerusalem; and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins.
output: Y salían a él toda la región de Judea, y todos los de Jerusalén; y eran bautizados por él en el río Jordán, confesando sus pecados.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And John was clothed in camel’s hair and had a leather girdle around his loins, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
output: Y Juan estaba vestido de pelo de camello, y tenía un cinto de cuero alrededor de sus lomos; y comía langostas y miel silvestre.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And he preached, saying, He who is stronger than I comes after me, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.
output: Y predicaba, diciendo: Viene tras mí el que es más fuerte que yo, a quien no soy digno de desatar, agachado, la correa de Sus sandalias.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: I have baptized you in water, but He Himself will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.
output: Yo os he bautizado en agua; pero Él os bautizará en el Espíritu Santo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And in those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized in the Jordan by John.
output: Aconteció en aquellos días, que Jesús vino de Nazaret de Galilea, y fue bautizado por Juan en el Jordán.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately, coming up out of the water, He saw the heavens being parted and the Spirit as a dove descending upon Him.
output: E inmediatamente, cuando subía del agua, vio abrirse los cielos y al Espíritu descender como paloma sobre Él.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And a voice came out of the heavens: You are My Son, the Beloved; in You I have found My delight.
output: Y vino una voz de los cielos que decía: Tú eres Mi Hijo, el Amado; en Ti me complazco.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately the Spirit thrust Him out into the wilderness.
output: En seguida el Espíritu le impulsó al desierto.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild animals, and the angels ministered to Him.
output: Y estuvo en el desierto cuarenta días, siendo tentado por Satanás; y estaba con las fieras, y los ángeles le ministraban.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And after John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the gospel of God,
output: Después que Juan fue entregado, Jesús vino a Galilea proclamando el evangelio de Dios,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And saying, The time is fulfilled and the kingdom of God has drawn near. Repent and believe in the gospel.
output: y diciendo: El tiempo se ha cumplido, y el reino de Dios se ha acercado; arrepentíos, y creed en el evangelio.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And passing beside the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew, Simon’s brother, casting a net in the sea, for they were fishermen.
output: Pasando junto al mar de Galilea, vio a Simón y a Andrés, hermano de Simón, echando la red en el mar; porque eran pescadores.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Jesus said to them, Come after Me, and I will cause you to become fishers of men.
output: Y les dijo Jesús: Venid en pos de Mí, y haré que seáis pescadores de hombres.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately leaving the nets, they followed Him.
output: Y dejando al instante las redes, le siguieron.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And going on a little, He saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, who also were in their boat, mending the nets.
output: Al ir un poco más adelante, vio a Jacobo hijo de Zebedeo, y a Juan su hermano, los cuales también estaban en la barca, remendando las redes.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately He called them, and leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, they went after Him.
output: Y al instante los llamó; y dejando a su padre Zebedeo en la barca con los jornaleros, se fueron en pos de Él.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they went into Capernaum, and immediately, on the Sabbath, He entered into the synagogue and taught.
output: Y entraron en Capernaum; e inmediatamente, el Sábado, entró en la sinagoga y se puso a enseñar.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they were astounded at His teaching, for He taught them as One having authority and not like the scribes.
output: Y se admiraban de Su enseñanza; porque les enseñaba como quien tiene autoridad, y no como los escribas.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately, there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out, saying,
output: Y de repente, en la sinagoga de ellos, un hombre con espíritu inmundo gritó, diciendo:

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: What have we to do with You, Jesus, Nazarene? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are — the Holy One of God.
output: ¿Qué tenemos que ver contigo, Jesús nazareno? ¿Has venido para destruirnos? Sé quién eres, el Santo de Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Be quiet and come out of him!
output: Pero Jesús le reprendió, diciendo: ¡Cállate, y sal de él!

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and shouting with a loud voice, came out of him.
output: Y el espíritu inmundo, sacudiéndole con violencia, y clamando a gran voz, salió de él.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they were all amazed, so that they discussed among themselves, saying, What is this? A new teaching! With authority He orders even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.
output: Y todos se asombraron, de tal manera que discutían entre sí, diciendo: ¿Qué es esto? ¡Una enseñanza nueva! Él manda con autoridad a los espíritus inmundos, y le obedecen.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately the report concerning Him went out everywhere into all the surrounding region of Galilee.
output: Y muy pronto se difundió Su fama por todas partes en la región alrededor de Galilea.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately, going out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew with James and John.
output: Inmediatamente después de salir de la sinagoga, entraron en la casa de Simón y Andrés, con Jacobo y Juan.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Simon’s mother-in-law was lying down with a fever, and immediately they told Him about her.
output: Y la suegra de Simón estaba acostada, con fiebre; y en seguida le hablaron de ella.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He came to her and raised her up, holding her hand, and the fever left her, and she served them.
output: Entonces Él se acercó, y la levantó, tomándola de la mano; y la fiebre la dejó, y ella se puso a servirles.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And when evening fell and the sun had set, they brought to Him all those who were ill and those who were demon-possessed.
output: Al atardecer, puesto ya el sol, le trajeron todos los enfermos y los endemoniados;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
output: y toda la ciudad estaba agolpada a la puerta.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases, and He cast out many demons and did not allow the demons to speak, because they knew Him.
output: Y sanó a muchos que padecían diversas enfermedades, y echó fuera muchos demonios; y no permitía a los demonios hablar, porque le conocían.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And rising very early in the morning, while it was still night, He went out and went away to a deserted place, and there He prayed.
output: Levantándose muy de mañana, siendo aún muy oscuro, salió y se fue a un lugar desierto, y allí oraba.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Simon and those with him hunted for Him,
output: Y Simón y los que estaban con él salieron en Su busca;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they found Him and said to Him, All are seeking You.
output: y hallándole, le dijeron: Todos te buscan.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He said to them, Let us go elsewhere into the nearby towns that I may preach there also, because for this purpose I came out.
output: Él les dijo: Vamos a otro lugar, a los pueblos vecinos, para que predique también allí; porque para esto he salido.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He went preaching in their synagogues in the whole of Galilee and casting out the demons.
output: E iba predicando en las sinagogas de ellos en toda Galilea y echando fuera los demonios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And a leper came to Him, entreating Him and falling on his knees and saying to Him, If You are willing, You can cleanse me.
output: Vino a Él un leproso, rogándole; y arrodillándose, le dijo: Si quieres, puedes limpiarme.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He was moved with compassion, and stretching out His hand, He touched him and said to him, I am willing; be cleansed!
output: Y Jesús, movido a compasión, extendió la mano y le tocó, y le dijo: Quiero, sé limpio.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And immediately the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.
output: Y al instante la lepra se fue de aquél, y quedó limpio.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And sternly charging him, immediately He thrust him out,
output: Y encargándole rigurosamente, en seguida le despidió,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And said to him, See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.
output: y le dijo: Mira que no digas nada a nadie, sino ve, muéstrate al sacerdote, y ofrece por tu purificación lo que Moisés ordenó, para testimonio a ellos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But he went out and began to proclaim it much and to spread the matter abroad, so that He could no longer enter openly into a city, but He stayed outside in deserted places. And they came to Him from everywhere.
output: Pero él salió y comenzó a proclamarlo mucho y a divulgar el hecho, de manera que Jesús ya no podía entrar abiertamente en ninguna ciudad, sino que se quedaba fuera en los lugares desiertos; y venían a Él de todas partes.
